title: Copy/paste or duplicate and drag/drop Workflow Actions
excerpt:
As a “no code” visual platform, I appreciate the way Workflows introduces additional logic to automations that was not there previously.

posts.author: jbreen3ki (JJ Breen)
posts.cookedHtml:
As a “no code” visual platform, I appreciate the way Workflows introduces additional logic to automations that was not there previously.

One situation I run into frequently is that I will have multiple branches of a workflow that require different logic and handling, but all end in a similar result with a notification email of a success or an error message in the item’s conversation.

Because Workflows does not allow for a side branch that rejoins the main flow (the way programming might), each branch ends up with its own final action.

Workflows also doesn’t allow rich copy/paste that preserves tags within an email or notification body (automations uses double curly braces {{ / }} for this).

I would like to be able to copy and paste or duplicate an action so that I can append it to the end of each workflow branch and either use it as-is or make the small modification to it that it needs to account for that specific workflow.

If that’s not possible, at least allow for rich copy/paste of tags to make it easier to duplicate notifications and email bodies that contain tag references.

posts.author: Clarey (MondayMotivator)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey, @jbreen3ki have you tried right clicking and duplicating the whole workflow and then making the small modification to it that it needs to account for that specific workflow? Perhaps its a Feature request if this was not what your looking for?

posts.author: jbreen3ki (JJ Breen)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi, @Clarey .
The issue is that the workflow branches with Condition statements that depend on certain statuses being filled out, blank, etc.

Successful branches result in a success message being sent to the user, failed branches log an error.

I want to duplicate an individual action block, such as “Create update”, which is already populated with the correct item tag, user tags, etc. rather than manually re-entering all of that information in a new action block, renaming the action block with something useful and descriptive, etc.

If I were to use separate workflows to accomplish this, that would be almost functionally the same as using the existing Automation functionality and setting up separate automations for each and every status type/combination, which is a maintenance nightmare.

I definitely forgot to add the “feature request” tag to this post, but I can’t seem to edit it to add that.
